name: t3-signinservice-passkey-tests
primary_metric: SignInService (high-risk, previously zero direct tests) gains deterministic device-free unit coverage of its passkey/WebAuthn surface — config logic, not-configured guard/error paths, persisted token-type contract, UserSession Codable round-trip.

success_metrics.primary: 10 SignInServicePasskeyTests covering canShowPasskeyLogin invariants + SessionTokenType raw-value/Codable persistence contract + UserSession passkey Codable round-trip; 10/10 pass on simulator.
secondary: Mirrors AuthManagerTests device-free scope (ASAuthorizationController happy-path device-only, documented)
Pins the persisted SessionTokenType raw values against silent rename drift

kill_criteria: Tests assert against the ASAuthorizationController UI flow (would be non-deterministic / device-only).
Tests pin implementation detail rather than the behavioral/persistence contract.
kill_criteria_resolution: Neither fired. Tests cover only the deterministic device-free surface (config props, unconfigured guard/error paths, enum/Codable contracts) — the ASAuthorizationController registration/assertion flow is explicitly out of scope (device-only, documented in the test header like AuthManagerTests documents real-device biometrics). The SessionTokenType raw-value tests pin a PERSISTENCE contract (stored sessions) not an implementation detail.
dispatch_pattern: operator-driven (T3 from test-coverage master plan; highest-risk zero-coverage service)

tasks.summary: Scope SignInService passkey surface (config props, guards, enums, UserSession) + AuthManagerTests template
Author SignInServicePasskeyTests.swift (10 env-independent device-free tests; rewrite after first run revealed the test bundle ships a real relying-party ID)

prd.note: b_medium; scope in test-coverage plan T3
